name: dnsmasq-base
title: dnsmasq-base
summary: Small caching DNS proxy and DHCP/TFTP server - executable
description:
Small caching DNS proxy and DHCP/TFTP server - executable
This package contains the dnsmasq executable and documentation, but
not the infrastructure required to run it as a system daemon. For
that, install the dnsmasq package.

commands.name: dnsmasq
commands.description: A lightweight DHCP and caching DNS server.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use dnsmasq because Small caching DNS proxy and DHCP/TFTP server - executable.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use dnsmasq as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `dnsmasq [OPTION]...` as documented upstream for dnsmasq.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair dnsmasq with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `dnsmasq --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.readme: dnsmasq - A lightweight DHCP and caching DNS server.
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install dnsmasq. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `dnsmasq [OPTION]...`. Small caching DNS proxy and DHCP/TFTP server - executable.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: Small caching DNS proxy and DHCP/TFTP server - executable.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: dnsmasq - A lightweight DHCP and caching DNS server.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install dnsmasq
Install with: sudo apt install dnsmasq
